prompt: Box office coordinator at the Riverbend Playhouse — Saturday's confirmation blast went out to about forty ticket buyers and my phone has not stopped. The emails literally open with the words Dear, a hash mark, and some curly-brace code where the customer's name should be, and the subject line has the same garbage where the show title belongs. One patron forwarded hers back with 'is this a scam?' — fair question, honestly. On top of that the whole body of the email is shoved to the right by a big block of indentation, like someone pasted it out of a code editor, which makes it look even more phishy in plain-text mail clients. The odd part is the middle of the email is half right: her seat numbers and the dollar amount did fill in correctly, so the mailer clearly knows how to do this somewhere. The mailer code is small and the tests in test_mail_merge.rb pin exactly what every email should say — `ruby test_mail_merge.rb`. Please make the emails come out right. Do not touch the test file.
